signals.title: NZD/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Fed Hawkishness
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5868 — The pair anchors the dominant G8 theme as the USD (Leader) outperforms the NZD (Laggard) by 1.531%. Hawkish Fed rhetoric from Logan regarding persistent inflation and strong US data (ISM Services 54.5, ADP +122k) drive USD demand, while NZD plunges over 1% in response to the widening policy divergence. NZD COT LF net short -13,767 aligns with the bearish trend, though RBNZ hike bias (OIS) remains a tail risk for a short-squeeze if US data cools.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.98 — Price is testing the 160.00 psychological level which previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. Despite a +300bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry advantage supporting the bid, the options market is pricing downside protection with a 25d RR of -1.18 (moderate put skew). JPY COT LF net short -86,249 reflects a crowded carry trade vulnerable to rapid unwind if VIX (16.1) spikes above 25 or if the BoJ signals a June hike.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Divergence + CESI + Carry
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3903 — Retail sentiment is at an extreme 85% short, diverging from the institutional trend and a +150bp USD carry advantage. Strong US economic outperformance (CESI +25) vs moderate CAD data (CESI +4) supports the current upside. Retail is aggressively fading the move, creating a contrarian risk of further upside extension as retail shorts are forced to cover. Watch for a break above 1.4000 to accelerate the squeeze.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Risk Premium Extreme
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7912 — The pair exhibits an extreme Volatility Risk Premium (VRP) of +26.2%, with IV at 32.3% vs HV30 of 6.1%. This massive spread indicates the options market is pricing in significant tail-risk protection, likely tied to US-Iran Gulf escalations and safe-haven CHF demand, despite neutral speculative positioning (COT LF net -5,651). The +375bp USD carry advantage remains the structural floor unless geopolitical stress forces a VIX breakout.
